intro: Porting code from JavaScript to C requires moving from a high-level, single-threaded runtime environment to a bare-metal, compiled language. Developers execute this extreme shift to optimize resource-constrained embedded hardware, author low-level system extensions, or eliminate garbage collection latencies. This migration requires abandoning dynamic objects in favor of explicit manual memory layouts.

diffs.from: Dynamic type definitions allocating variables freely at runtime with pervasive type coercion rules.
diffs.to: Static, weakly checked primitive types where structural changes require explicit memory offset remapping.

diffs.from: Modern C-derivative script layout featuring flexible object literals, array syntax, and arrow function definitions.
diffs.to: Rigid C standard structure requiring explicit function headers, strict type definitions, and manual management of pointer symbols.

diffs.from: Fully automated tracking and cleanup managed dynamically by a high-performance engine garbage collector.
diffs.to: Manual memory lifecycle control using allocation and release functions (malloc, calloc, free) risking memory corruption bugs.

diffs.from: Non-blocking, event loop-driven architecture prioritizing asynchronous task completions and native Promises.
diffs.to: Synchronous linear execution flows; parallel architecture requires direct invocation of low-level OS threading primitives like POSIX threads.

diffs.from: Built around high-level APIs for string parsing, complex web workers, cryptographic tools, and DOM/Node environments.
diffs.to: Minimalist header collection focusing tightly on raw system inputs, file operations, math macros, and basic string buffers.

examples.title: String Concatenation vs Buffer Management
examples.description: Transforming dynamic high-level string joining into safe C character array buffer creation.

examples.title: Iterating an Array
examples.description: Mapping a dynamic JavaScript collection loop to a standard static pointer block index loop.

tips.title: Track Collection Capacities
tips.body: C arrays cannot automatically grow or track their own lengths. You must always pass explicit size tracking arguments alongside array data buffers.

tips.title: Perform Manual Memory Audits
tips.body: Ensure every execution path through a block allocating memory with malloc contains a matching, single invocation of the free function.

tips.title: Replace Dynamic Objects with Structs
tips.body: Map JavaScript key-value dictionaries into static C structs with predefined primitive data fields.

tips.title: Eliminate Closure Patterns
tips.body: Functions in C cannot trap local scope state variables. Convert all closures into standalone functions that accept explicit state pointers.

faqs.question: What happens if I read past an array index in C?
faqs.answer: Unlike JavaScript which safely returns 'undefined', C reads raw unallocated memory addresses. This triggers undefined behavior or crashes the program with a segmentation fault.

faqs.question: How do I handle JSON strings in C?
faqs.answer: C has no built-in object parser. You must import an external open-source utility like cJSON to manually tokenize and walk through text documents.

faqs.question: Is there a direct equivalent to 'null'?
faqs.answer: Yes, C uses the NULL macro, which evaluates numerically to 0. It indicates an uninitialized or empty memory reference pointer.
